mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales indicate strengthening consumer demand, which directly boosts corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of months with rising RSAFS.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the federal funds rate signals aggressive monetary tightening, which immediately raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of instances following a federal funds rate increase exceeding 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the unemployment rate and increases in total nonfarm payrolls confirm a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when UNRATE falls but PAYEMS growth remains below 100k, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the index of industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity and robust supply-side momentum, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns during periods of inventory overhang or when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate earnings and forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL acceleration correlates with positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of observations, or Rank IC remains negative over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts indicates a sudden collapse in construction demand and consumer confidence, triggering immediate risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX yields positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of months following a housing starts decline exceeding 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with a flattening or rising unemployment rate signal policy-induced economic slowdown, which suppresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction effect reverses sign when FEDFUNDS rises but UNRATE remains below 4.0%, or the joint predictor shows no out-of-sample alpha.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth reflects broad-based economic expansion that supports corporate earnings revisions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns when accompanied by rising input costs or when annualized GDP growth exceeds 4.0%.
